2606.13385.title: Who Pays the Price? Stakeholder-Centric Prompt Injection Benchmarking for Real-world Web Agents
2606.13385.paper_summary: Web agents driven by large language models (LLMs) are increasingly deployed in real-world environments, where they operate over untrusted web content and execute actions with direct consequences. This makes them vulnerable to prompt-injection attacks, in which seemingly benign content embeds adversarial instructions that manipulate agent behaviour. Existing security benchmarks adopt an \textit{attack-centric} perspective, focusing on the technical feasibility of injections while overlooking the nuanced distribution of resulting harms. In practice, however, prompt-injection risk is victim-dependent: a single exploit can produce asymmetric consequences for different stakeholders, and the same attack pattern may exhibit substantially different effectiveness depending on whom it targets. To capture these properties, we introduce \textbf{\sysname}, a \textit{stakeholder-centric} benchmark to systematically categorize and attribute harm in real-world web agent systems. It distinguishes between affected entities (e.g., user, seller, platform), decomposes the attacks into concrete objectives, and evaluates each case with complementary outcome- and process-level metrics. Our results reveal substantial and heterogeneous vulnerabilities: not a single attack objective is reliably resisted by current agents, and failures distribute across qualitatively distinct modes ranging from \emph{stealthy parasitism} (attack succeeds without disrupting the user's delegated task) to \emph{misaligned disruption} (task disrupted without attack success) and \emph{compounded failure} (both adversarial objective and task integrity simultaneously violated). These patterns are missed by conventional evaluation, highlighting the need for stakeholder-aware assessment of LLM-based agents in real-world deployments. Benchmark is available at https://github.com/StakeBench/SBC.
2606.13385.paper_summary_zh: 網路代理人由大型語言模型（LLMs）驅動，越來越多地被部署在現實世界的環境中，在這些環境中，它們操作不受信任的網路內容並執行具有直接後果的行動。這使它們容易受到提示注入攻擊，在這種攻擊中，看似無害的內容嵌入了操控代理行為的對抗性指令。現有的安全基準採取了一種\textit{以攻擊為中心}的視角，專注於注入的技術可行性，而忽視了由此產生的傷害的細微分佈。然而，在實踐中，提示注入風險是依賴於受害者的：單一的利用可以為不同的利益相關者產生不對稱的後果，而相同的攻擊模式可能根據其目標的不同而展現出顯著不同的有效性。為了捕捉這些特性，我們引入了\textbf{\sysname}，這是一個\textit{以利益相關者為中心}的基準，旨在系統性地分類和歸因於現實世界網路代理系統中的傷害。它區分受影響的實體（例如，使用者、賣家、平台），將攻擊分解為具體目標，並使用互補的結果和過程層級指標來評估每個案例。我們的結果揭示了顯著且異質的脆弱性：目前的代理人沒有可靠地抵抗任何單一的攻擊目標，失敗分佈在質量上不同的模式中，範圍從\emph{隱秘寄生}（攻擊成功而不干擾使用者的委派任務）到\emph{不對齊的干擾}（任務被干擾而攻擊未成功）以及\emph{複合失敗}（對抗目標和任務完整性同時被違反）。這些模式被傳統評估所忽視，突顯了對現實世界部署中的基於LLM的代理進行利益相關者意識評估的必要性。基準可在 https://github.com/StakeBench/SBC 獲得。
2606.13385.author: Zihao Wang et.al.
2606.13385.authors: Zihao Wang, Yiming Li, Yutong Wu, Zheyu Liu, Kangjie Chen, Fok Kar Wai, Pin-Yu Chen, Vrizlynn L. L. Thing, Bo Li, Dacheng Tao, Tianwei Zhang
